smsAutomation.title: SMS automation
smsAutomation.permissionRequiredMessage: Auto-uploading verification codes needs SMS receive permission. Please allow it in system settings.
smsAutomation.enabledToast: Auto-upload of SMS verification codes enabled
smsAutomation.disabledToast: Auto-upload of SMS verification codes disabled

clipboardAccess.title: Clipboard access

enableSection.footer: iOS only shows the "Allow Paste" prompt—and remembers this permission—after the app has read content copied from another app at least once. On a fresh install, before you've ever triggered it, the "Paste from Other Apps" row won't even appear in system settings. Tap the button below to trigger it once: when "Allow Paste" appears, choose "Allow" to grant the permission, and you won't be asked again.

clipboardAccess.triggeredStep: Read triggered once. Tap "Allow" when the prompt appears.

fallbackSection.header: No prompt, or want to change your choice
fallbackSection.footer: If no prompt appeared, the clipboard may be empty (copy some text first, then trigger), or you previously tapped "Don't Allow" and iOS remembered it. Go to system settings and set "Paste from Other Apps" to "Allow"—this row only appears after you've triggered it once. iOS doesn't expose the current value to the app, so there's no need to return and confirm after changing it.

relatedSection.footer: When enabled, content copied on this device is automatically read and pushed; we recommend setting the permission above to "Allow" first. Leave it off to push manually with the "Paste" button on the home screen—iOS won't prompt.
